taxonomy: Vignea × gaudiniana is a hybrid species of grapevine. It is a cross between Vitis vinifera and Vitis gaudichaudii. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic makeup. As a hybrid species, Vignea × gaudiniana is not assigned a rank in the traditional Linnaean system of classification. The specific classification of Vignea × gaudiniana is often considered as Vitis gaudichaudii or its close relative.
uses: Vignea × gaudiniana, also known as Riverbank grape or Wild Scuppernong, has been reported to have various medicinal properties. It is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
